title: Ballota forsskalii
taxonomy: Ballota forsskalii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Ballota, which comprises several species of shrubs and herbs. The exact classification of Ballota forsskalii may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic research. However, it is generally recognized as a plant native to the Mediterranean region. Ballota forsskalii is often classified as a perennial herb.

intro: Ballota forsskalii, also known as Forsskal's Horehound, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ballota forsskalii include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ballota forsskalii is often prepared as a tea or an infusion, and it can also be found in various commercial herbal supplements and cough syrups.
